channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: /Biz revival — 114 replies — 130.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
/Biz revival

Open thread • Replies: 114 • Updated: 2026-05-26 00:30 UTC

OP

while this board is clearly dead and all we have left is 10 anons, one of which is a aid devastated bald whiny fag, i think this is the perfect time to try to revive this board.

"How"? i'm hearing you ask.
Well. Let's make this a general where we only share our best picks. NO pointless SHILLING, either stocks or the occasional good crypto you really believe in.


I'll start.

Litecoin is now around 50$. This is a generational bottom if you look on the multi year chart. A solid support. At this price, unless you're convinced altcoins and cryptos are over, it's a free lottery ticket. In the same spirit, LITS, a company which treasury is only composed of its litecoin holding is also a great way to get litecoin exposure.

Ton, the crypto of one if not the most used chatting app worldwide is going back to a historical bottom as we speak after doing a small pump following several changes and pavel durov's statement. (20% staking apy, many other changes planned i won't go into details but good narrative)

A risky stock play but with very good risk / reward ratio: UBISOFT, company went to shit, but they are restructuring and trying to turn things around. A potential 10 bagger over the next years if they can achieve that, and they definitively can if they stop their bullshit.

Early replies (first 3)

62265475
>>62265467
Just buy OpenAI at IPO.
All normalfags will do that and outperform your "smart" plays.
There's such a thing as being too smart.

62265476
>>62265474
yeah that's precisely what we're trying to avoid you fucking retard. Stay poor

62265480
but what if I am bald?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62267267
>>62267090
i'll look into it but it's already 2x / 3x from bottom

62267325
>>62267267
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q4vF3o4Ss6g

62267354
>>62265467
Lillybux is the next /biz/coin

62267369
>>62265467
greetings anon i have just returned from a hiatus therefore the board is alive again. the only coin you need now is $PUMP. the irony is $PUMP killed crypto by making it possible to launch so many coins that there became no community like there used to be, so now we have to invert that into wealth by buying PUMP. you'll see. bull run hasn't even begun.

62267402
Every single factor that led to GME and CAR going parabolic is perfectly aligned for GRPN in 6 days.

62267687
>>62265467
I regret selling my litecoin and I've bought some of it back. It has a strong narrative as OG coin. There's the livVM being worked on which will bring eth style smart contracts to it. IDK how decentralized it will be but it's a start.

ICP. Do yourself a favor and actually read some of the tech details of how it works. They're setting up "sovereign networks" signing deals with heads of state. Prepping for the future. Chainkey cryptography is wizardry.
The biggest downside, IMO, is the UI is pretty shit - you have to sign a lot of "canisters" (smart contracts) to get going. But it's the only tech that combines blockchain with website plus is reasonably distributed.

62269707
>>62267687
litecoin has developped MWEB network, which is basically private transactions integrated into the already ltc blockchain. it's a pretty big deal, like an integrated coin mixer that is fully private (can't decipher it just like monero).
Very few apps use it yet.
Once it finally gets some sufficient support on any ltc wallet, it will be much like zcash. You go into MWEB, and every public trace stop there. you can also go out of mweb of course.

62269750
Is everyone here stuck in 2017? How about a coin that has come out in the past half decade gramps.

62270867
Ntdoy and rare earth etfs. Some uranium too (nuclear nrgy not nukes)

62270942
I find it so hard to browse this board these days, the humor is so so tiring. Just the same jokes over and over again. I know some people say they find gems here every know and then but I never have, anyone got any advice? I’m so tired man

62271126
>>62265467
Why buy LITS instead of LTC?

62271298
>>62271126
because it's like buying litecoin with a discount, and when ltc pumps LITS will pump multiple X more just like micro strategy did with btc

62271371
>>62265467
>Ton
Turns out that's in my bookmarks as something with potential. I looked at Litecoin when I first got into crypto, but I figured these long-standing projects like it, LINK, HBAR, etc. were things I'd come back to later with gains from lower market cap stuff. Seems that was the correct decision since so many of them are now around the same price as they day I bought my first assets.
I figured they might make great strides someday but not in a hurry since they'd already make great strides (price-wise) since their debuts. Despite that I'm shocked to see Litecoin is like half the price of when I first looked at it.
I'd recommend some stuff myself but I'm still in that newish area where I've made good picks after reading up on stuff before buying (the DYOR part /biz/ always told me to do) but couldn't briefly articulate why someone should take a look at something at current prices. Everything that's done me well is more expensive now than when I first bought it.

62271377
>>62265877
I don't think there's a LUNC thread up now but I remember seeing one recently where there was a lot of reasonable optimism.

62271378
>>62265467
It's dead Jim

62271387
>>62270867
What's the basis for uranium at this time? I saw a few months ago people talking about various reactors, from new mini ones to restarting some existing ones.

62271407
>>62271387
an anon shilled some us stock, nano nuclear energy and i didn't buy because the chart looked already pumped. it went down a lot since like 40%

Nano nuclear energy. I looked it up they're trying to develop mini nuclear reactors thant can be moved around, like for data centers etc. But i'm wondering isn't that tech super risky?in any case that might or might not be a great moonshot. No idea, don't own any but looking at it

62271421
>>62271407
I can see what you would be. I remember seeing some airplane-lifted reactors some months ago. Operation Windlord. Valar Atomics, though, they are not publicly traded.
I see what you mean about NNE having doing about a 10x since it began and now being like 50% down since then. Still, nice to have specific leads on what to research.

62271501
>>62265467
Its dead because there havent been enough nigger days due to the bull market

62271653
How about starting with removing this faggot nigger captcha and having to wait for a minute for it? I gave up posting here, because of it, honestly. And I'm not verifying my email each time either.

62271675
>>62271653
Captchas become faster and easier if you post more

62271760
>>62271675
and then reset your progress for no reason
no, I didn't erase my cookies, just couldn't post for TWO DAYS

62271767
>>62271760
yes jannies trannies destroyed it all. it's over. At least biz is the only white board left

62271790
>>62265467
good pick with litecoin anon, literally the only coin which can reliably process frequent micropayments without clogging or taking too many fees


it will be the coin of choice for autonomous AI agents to transact with eachother in the future

62271899
>>62265467
what killed this place to begin with is that there is no business or finance talk going on here. Its just retarded "buy this crypto coin!!!"

62272124
>>62265467
>LTC
Abandoned project. $50 stable ass coin.

>TON
Shit is never going to pump.

>UBISOFT, company went to shit, but they are restructuring and trying to turn things around. A potential 10 bagger over the next years if they can achieve that, and they definitively can if they stop their bullshit.
>IF
They won't ever back down their bullshit, even in the deepest shit they still prefered to appeal to niggers and fags instead of making games.

62272342
>>62272124
>Abandoned project. $50 stable ass coin.
they just developped MWEB transactions anon. Try harder.

why wouldn't ton pump? are you retarded?

nvgmi

62272366
>>62265467
india and china need to be banned from this board

62273281
>>62271298
Seems like it crashes X times more too unfortunately. Maybe a good idea for ltc exposure but I’m really unsure if litecoin will be the play next bull

62273290
>>62265467
>we need to revive biz
>how do we do it you ask
>well its simple you just buy my decade old bags of a shitcoin so i can finally break even
well guess we found aid devastated bald whiny fag and its op

62272176

item.title: I wish LINK had never come to me. I wish none of this had happened. — 5 replies — 20.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
I wish LINK had never come to me. I wish none of this had happened.

Open thread • Replies: 5 • Updated: 2026-05-26 01:20 UTC

OP

I wish LINK had never come to me. I wish none of this had happened.

Early replies (first 3)

62272779
>>62272707
don't worry, you'll be "happy" when it "moons" to $100 in another 20 years

62272949
bilbo bagheld for 60 years and then gave his treasure away. but unlike link marines, he could use the ring for something better than putting it in a piggy bank with a number that goes up

62273063
https://github.com/Direct-Private-Offers/dtcc-sto-upgraded-latest/blob/main/contracts/integration/EuroclearBridge.sol#L22
line 64
sorry for giving you hope

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62273286
>>62273063
Lmao, didn't even open the link but I can assure you that you are possibly one of the stupidest people browsing this board.

62273379
So do all who come to hold such bags, but that is not for them to decide. All we have to decide is what to do with the bags that were offloaded on us.

item.title: Attainable careers/skills for money — 4 replies — 16.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Attainable careers/skills for money

Open thread • Replies: 4 • Updated: 2026-05-26 01:20 UTC

OP

Everywhere you look they see it’s not worth it to do or train for xyz job especially if it’s tech or admin related
But it’s hard to even tell what is true or not anymore some just want to gatekeep or fear monger
Originally I wanted to get into UI/UX design but I keep hearing it’s getting more and more reduced to a function than a full position thanks to AI
Please help is there something no BS I can learn with actual gainful employment at the end and possibly to work for myself or freelance?

Early replies (first 3)

62273250
>>62273242
med or law

everything else is shit really, high finance is good but you need nepo connections. /med/ is the ultimate equalizer, if you are competent and do good u will make a shitload of money in a high risk specialty

62273265
fuck off find your niche

62273376
I became an accountant because of a biz thread posted here in 2023

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62273378
>>62273242
Pressure washer.

item.title: Audemars Piguet x Swatch — 19 replies — 11.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
Audemars Piguet x Swatch

Open thread • Replies: 19 • Updated: 2026-05-25 23:41 UTC

OP

What does /biz think about reselling the Audemars Piguet x Swatch “Royal Pop”?

How much have you made from it, or how much do you expect to make?

I bought 3 “Royal Pop” watches and have already sold 2 - the winner is around 1,000 US Dollar for both watches.

Early replies (first 3)

62266200
>>62266191
It’s chump change desu, I’m not going to bother for $1,000.

gg though.

62266205
>>62266191
They look like happy meal toys.

62266218
>>62266200
How much money do you make and how?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62266245
>>62266191
I don't buy goyslop

62266257
I'm not a consumerist subhuman goy like you OP
go back to dealing in the real world

62266259
>>62266218
Variable, but enough to be unbothered about going to some physical shop, trying to sell the stuff, dealing with dumbass customers, shipping, etc. for only $1,000 in the end.

I’m an advisor for billionaires and major companies.

62266457
Woah, is that le heckin jewelry that I can use to show other insecure homosexuals my net worth?

62266499
>>62266191
>"bioceramic"
Kek this is the jewish shill way of saying plastic

Its not bad to flip the watches I guess, its probably the same demographic as the Pokémon scalpers.
I saw that some people were selling "presales" of chinese made bracelets for the watches for hundreds of dollars to normies fomoing on ebay

62266530
>>62266191
It is amazing how AP has driven their prestige into the ground over the last decade or so.

62266537
Being a watch fag is cringe

Pic related all you need

62266572
>>62266530
>AP
Audemars Piguet cost at least tens of thousands of US Dollars.
Now anyone can buy an AP, which is why there's so much hype surrounding the Royal Pop watch.

62266738
>>62266572
Your pic captures my point better than the Swatch collab.

62266779
>>62266738

The negro in this pictures is a well-known musician he call himself "50 Cent" and he is wearing a 235`000 US Dollar Audemars Piguet.

Many other hip-hop artists mention the watch brand in their songs, but fans haven't been able to afford the brand so far - That's Royal Pop's target audience

62266806
>>62266779
(You) are Indian. The Matterhorn pic doesn’t fool me

62266845
>>62266806No, I don't relieve myself outdoors.

Swatch manufactures the Royal Pop fully automatically in Switzerland; no watchmakers are needed for production, only robots.

This means the Royal Pop can be produced in very large quantities at very low cost.

Audemars Piguet is foregoing profits from the Royal Pop.

Swatch will make a lot of money from this. Unlike previous collaborations, Audemars Piguet is the first prestigious watch brand outside the Swatch Group.

62269732
>>62266191
they look cute
My girlfirend is one of those quirky girls with a backpack full of labubus and pins maybe i buy her one

62271174
>>62269732
Lan Ba is the most sought-after color.
Right now, people are paying several thousand US Dollar on the secondary market for this color.
You'd be better off waiting a few more months until everyone can buy the Royal Pop for 400 US Dollar at any Swatch store.

62273145
Can I remove the words "Royal Pop" off the back? That's so fucking gay. Otherwise might be a nice pocketwatch looks sturdy.

62273173
how do you make sure you don’t get ripped off reselling them? isn’t it mostly nogs buying?

62271355

MODSSSSSSSSSS

62271450

stfu, icy piss isn't for curry loving indian niggers, it's the mooninite choice

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62271475
please fuck my ass

62271604
>>62271355
Who hurt you Saar?

I will never stop snail posting ICP

62272095
>>62271450
stfu faggot

62272461
>>62271237
/pol/ brainlets btfo, ok? Moonman scam finally getting reveal

62272524
>>62272461
Are you the same person from like ever fucking day? You’re just repeating this moonman shit like it matters. Fuck off

62272530
>>62272524
I’ll keep posting it until someone posts the vocaroo

62272548
>>62272461
Bros I still can’t believe it, that Moonman would do this to us…he he was racist! I just don’t understand, how could he scam us like this as a racist? The feeling of euphoria I had when I put my life savings into ICP at $300, Moonman had just made a thread and said nigger in it repeatedly, I was saying nigger while I was buying and now….

62273253
>>62271237
icp 30 usd soon

item.title: I finally realized I "can't relax" because I just love working more than leisure. — 11 replies — 7.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
I finally realized I "can't relax" because I just love working more than leisure.

Open thread • Replies: 11 • Updated: 2026-05-25 23:58 UTC

OP

I finally realized I "can't relax" because I just love working more than leisure. What's wrong with that? I think it's something built into my white DNA.

Early replies (first 3)

62273038
>>62273016
Then work, but eventually you WILL stop when your passive income beats what most jobs pay.

62273160
>>62273016
work for god

62273167
Interesting. I can't work because I just love the feeling of spurting cum out of my penis too much to ever do anything else.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62273177
>>62273167
Try squirting into your own mouth. Heavenly.

62273188
>>62273016
I used to be this way. It’s not work I don’t like. But modern work politics and the absolute ass raping of taxes in the $100-200k per year income range. They’ve made modern work only palpable to over socialized women and h1b slavers who dont say no

62273192
>>62273177
Sounds gross.

62273200
>>62273192
Not really. The warm squirt on the tongue feels good.

62273203
>>62273200

62273207
>>62273016
Are you of German ancestry?

62273208
>>62273016
I'm white and I "relax" and "eat snax" regularly

62273213
>>62273188
Move to a tax haven (sorry if you’re American though) and work for your own company

item.title: Guys, need help > You keep denying reality. — 8 replies — 6.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
Guys, need help > You keep denying reality.

Open thread • Replies: 8 • Updated: 2026-05-26 00:12 UTC

OP

Guys, need help

> You keep denying reality. Basic research boy. The top 10% are spending LESS.

I already explained to him the top 10% are spending more and more but he keeps saying I am wrong.

Tell me what to say.

Early replies (first 3)

62269967
>>62269964
schizo
stop making these threads and seek medical help

62270057
>>62269967
I need to reply to that person.
Answer the op.

62270068
>>62270057
tell him you had a schizophrenic breakdown you loser

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62270433
>>62270068
Who is the loser

62270975
>>62269964
>end of meritocracy
a return to meritocracy

62271045
>>62269964
Stop shitting up the board you turbo autist.

62273202
>>62270975
This.
Females are retarded.

62273248
>>62269964
The top 10% are spending LESS
this why they top 10%

item.title: It’s over PEPE, ICP has the high ground — 4 replies — 3.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
It’s over PEPE, ICP has the high ground

Open thread • Replies: 4 • Updated: 2026-05-26 00:06 UTC

OP

Early replies (first 3)

62272880
a computer??? a computer on the internet??? an internet computer???

62272891
>>62272880
YES, even retards can have computer now and go to the internet!

62272921
>>62272891
wait...wait...but you need a computer with internet to go onto the internet's computer ohhh my gawd how did they miss this detail

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62273232
>>62272846
ICP moon when?

item.title: What’s stopping you from buying BTC? — 48 replies — 31.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
What’s stopping you from buying BTC?

Open thread • Replies: 48 • Updated: 2026-05-25 23:50 UTC

OP

What is it? Fear? Lack of conviction? Waiting for lower prices? Or do you genuinely think Bitcoin fails long term?

Curious what’s holding people back at this stage.

Early replies (first 3)

62271721
>>62271712
Btc needs a catalyst. It probably will be yield curve control with a new name. It could also be codifying the SBR into law which is less likely. Or my personal favorite schizo theory, it's discovered that Iran was mining and selling bitcoin to fund it's endeavors and that their supply is finally exhausted.

62271922
>>62271712
Gays in it.

62271933
>>62271712
everybody and their femboy lovers above two-digits IQ are waiting for BTC to dump below at least 40k by July. Why anybody buy now? 4 years cycles never lie

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62272488
>>62272017
The focus is on stable coins specifically to sell to emerging markets and issuers buy US bonds with the proceeds. This would have been bullish for BTC as a rise in stable coin market cap typically leads to a rise in btc mc. However Trump decided to set the world on fire for Israel, you cannot earn a yield on stable coins but you can on treasuries or a hysa, third worlders won't buy stable coins because they are busy trying to buy flour and gasoline. They will have to suggest another grift, if they are adament about saving banks then crypto has to remain uncompetitive.

62272623
>>62272447
Not just settlement. More like BTC evolving into productive financial infrastructure through lending, liquidity access, treasury systems, and broader BTCFi use cases. What do you think?

62272628
>>62271922
Gays? You in?

62272632
>>62271933
People said the same thing at 20k, 30k, 40k, 60k and 70k too.

>At some point you either start accumulating or spend years waiting for the “perfect” entry that never comes.

62272643
>>62271712
I haven't even looked at the price in 6 months.

62272647
>>62271712
its a worse option than chip stocks. sorry.

62272659
>>62271960
too bad you didnt buy at $200 or you'd be retired. its not going from $200 to 100k equivalent again.

62272661
>>62272632
over the last 5 years chip stocks did far better. no reason outsized btc gains will return vs other investments.

62272667
>>62272050
(lack of)

62272672
>>62271712

62272678
too expensive atm
maybe when its under 20k again

62272700
>>62272482
>blockchain
>Ponzi

Choose one

62272709
>>62271712
Opportunity cost.
Why would I put money into Bitcoin when I could just put it into stock and options?

62272718
>>62272700
>thinks you need Bitcoin or crypto to have blockchain
that's about right for a crypto / bitcoin investor, doesn't know shit.

62272725
>>62272659
It’s only 500x and we can do it again, BTC can go lower in this bear market and then reach $10,000,000 in our lifetimes.

62272734
>>62272718
Excuse me, I didn’t realize I was talking to a retard.

>Maybe if it dropped -80% in price I'd think about it.
Yes, I am sure you invested all your money on BTC in the previous 70% to 90% drops.

62272803
>>62272725
So it drops by 30-60%, there would be plenty of buyers but in such an event they'd probably jump ship at 6 figures and they'd be lucky if thats reached short term and not over a period of years. In an environment where a supposedly risk off asset drops that much, the leveraged btc treasuries come into question as well as the broader economy. Most of crypto and equities recent historic gains came in a period liquidity injections in the market to keep the wheels moving and avoid a financial depression. The problem is debt to GDP ratios are now completely bliwn out, little of the money went to productive assets and it's starting to affect the bond market. The solution will be a continuation of money printing vis a vis yield control but it either goes towards manufacturing or the country ceases to exist. Most assets will be in the gutter and many, retail or not, will not have money to put into assets. Those that do would have to ignore gold and blue chip stocks on firesale to buy btc for 2-3x when greater gains and dividends can be had in the stock market. If fiscal dominance is not following a short term correction and everything runs hot, btc will not be as attractive as stocks underfunded sector like commodities and energy, the basis of the productive assets and the only way out of the inflationary spiral. BTC will go up, most stocks will that don't die to inflation and supply shocks but many will barely keep up with inflation. Stocks and any crypto would need to be up 10%-20% just to avoid inflation tax and many will not have excess income. The gold market is quietly becoming preferred currency for international settlement and Chinas means of undermining US embargos. If you're in crypto I wouldn't bet on 500x and be happy if any kept up with or surpassed gold both in relative price and price relative to inflation. The era of easy money is over, you want extreme gains, research stocks significantly under valued.

62272874
>>62272467
It's best use case is moving huge wealth out of a country the best example being the recent exodus from the gulf following the revelation that Iran remains a regional power if only through being able to hit anything with a missile. Portability is without match but kyc and anti money laundering systems are pervasive in every nation that isn't a near collapsing shithole. Its value as this sort of investment vehicle is tainted should a return to extreme volatility to the downside occur. A bullcase for BTC remains better if it doesn't under perform the broader market in a decline, certainly its not good if it under performs gold.

The unfortunate and boring truth with bitcoin is that if it has any future it won't go up and down wildly and if it outperforms stocks it still would be a modest outlier. Gromens borrowed 25% risk off, 25% stocks, 25% real estate/business & 25% cash or short term t-bills for a shock proof portfolio use to include BTC as a larger portion of his risk off assets and thats probably the place it should stay for anyone continuing to buy it who isn't planning to be a refugee. Its an analog to gold at best but gold buyers remain optimistic about golds relative future while BTC maxis have bifrocated views of extreme valuations based on personal desires or past performance or seeing it as a value play at some point in the future with a heavy discount.

I'd be more incline to buy btc if its proponents were closer to goldbulls but half the premise is that revaluing gold is of the few remaining tricks the government can pull to stay afloat. No gold bull who isn't a johnny come lately underwater from buying silver at peak, is making threads asking why is no one buying gold? Mostly because they have never stopped buying gold themselves.

62272881
>>62271712
better returns in boomer index funds and stocks. we are all here to make money. minority is actually interested in the technology. I rather buy tsmc than btc especially if you zoom out. It couldnt even outperform the s&p in the past 5 years despite having insane volatility.

62272915
>>62272725
>and then reach $10,000,000 in our lifetimes.
You're genuinely retarded if you think this would somehow be a good thing, since for that to happen a loaf of bread would likely be selling for $20,000.
Bitcoin does not, and never can, create value. It can only reallocate money, and the time when poor people could benefit from that was in 2007.

62272929
>>62272803
I don’t agree with most assumptions here, but interesting post desu. I’ll keep it in my head.

62272939
>>62272915
>calls other Anon retarded
>immediately spouts the most retarded hot take ever

Every single time lol.

62272980
>>62272734
>Excuse me, I didn’t realize I'm a retard.
ftfy

62272985
>>62271933
It's not going below 65, srry buddy

62272991
>>62272002
More likely than the world moving to yuan or usd...

62273010
>>62272980
No u

62273018
>>62272874
>It's best use case is moving huge wealth out of a country
can agree with that, once regular holders are not treated like criminals and self custody made illegal.

62273107
>>62272939
>hyperinflation is a hot take
Please keep donating all your money to people who are, and will always be, wealthier than you.

62273111
>>62272929
I've been wrong before but mostly get macro outlook from Rick Rule, Dalio (mostly for social political) and Luke Gromen. From these inflation is unavoidable being several times what the government will admit to and the stock market going broadly up is over. Stock picking will overperform everything if intelligent and crypto is at present sabotaged by everything the government is doing, even if they planned to bail themselves out short term using genius act for additional funding. Gromen was a BTC bull but sold relatively near the top at ~93k and got major shit for it but thought BTC is acting odd and crypto being hobbled by the US directly and indirectly is not particularly good looking for the near term. It would be different if BTC was used for international settlement like gold is currently but it has risks of being weaponized and seized. So long as the US wants to be retarded and use assets as weapons commodity producers and buyers will trend towards things that the US cannot manipulate, the underlying citizens do they same for the most part. Stable coins had a chance if the price of fuel and staple grains hadn't skyrocketted for the forseeable future and this likely would have been bullish for BTC.

62273196
>>62271712

62262852

62265362

62267557

62267594

62268019
I am aiming for having 0,1 BTC by the end of the year (got a nasty 3'000$ tax bill in august and september) but aside from that I'm going all in every month from now.

62268405

62268430

62268528

62269062

62269119

62269122

62269256

62269965
>>62246438

item.title: Why is US consumer sentiment so low — 43 replies — 16.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
Why is US consumer sentiment so low

Open thread • Replies: 43 • Updated: 2026-05-25 22:45 UTC

OP

Why is US consumer sentiment so low

Early replies (first 3)

62269461
>>62269455
>be burger
>vote retard
>retard do trade war and real war
>cost living up wtf

62269462
>>62269455
they have no money?

62269465
>>62269461
basically this

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62269614
>>62269461
>vote retard
What else was on the ballot?

62269636
>>62269614
A BLUE RETARD

62269639
it's just that time of month. i feel most business startups are just entertainment for the proles. the businesses that do matter and make an impact on advancing research costs a lot and is bound to recieve government attention. not everyone can be a rocket scientist. maybe a lot can, but only if they had all the resources and time, but as we know we are limited pretending not to. statistics don't lie that we're in a pinch. why not just become slaves again? why not concentrate all the resources? yes there is a lot of potential for the young, but why prioritize them when you have people that already know what they are doing? the young should realize this, for if not knowing how to struggle, the more resources they will drain anyways. i don't mean this to be ragebait btw, but when times are tough people tend to move to extremes and things get scary

62269641
>>62269610
>the greatest demonstration of why basic income would never work. Give everyone $1000 a month and everything just goes up in price to compensate.
the point is to fundamentally change how incentives are doled out. instead of giving massive subsidies to corporations that in turn waste money on AI and jack up prices on everything, the destitute formerly employed wagies are given money to buy food and pay rent.

62269643
Miracle economy

62269647
>>62269461
Jews control every voteable optio

62269718
>>62269455
AI taking all our jerbs

62269725
I went to canes, a notriously overpriced and overrated chicken place today at noon.

The drive through was packed and when I say packed there was 40+ cars in line. There were so many people that there was a line into the parking lot and then out of it into the other parking lot across the restaurant, with the cars having to make a space for traffic to pass.

On top of that they parking lot for canes was full and the restaurant was full.


So if people are so doom and gloom poor, complaining that everything is expensive why was there 70-80 people eating lunch at an overpriced chicken joint at max capacity

62269728
>>62269455
ONLY REAL ANSWER IN THE WHOLE FUCKING THREAD: the methodology changed, the survey is now mostly democrat, instead of being 50/50 democrat/republican. Actually the sentiment of either democrats and republicans has not changed for some time now... however if the % of democrats slowly goes up in the survey then you will see the consumer sentiment value drop over time.

62269747
>>62269461
>>vote retard
RETARDS ARE THE ONLY OPTIONS WE'RE EVER GIVEN

62269798
>>62269461
MIGA
I
G
A

62269874
>>62269455
Because people are cooked.
Something broke after covid, a lot of people stopped living in the real world and became citizens of the internet instead.
The economy is doing okay, maybe even good, but the doom and gloom that is pervasive on the internet, has led a lot of people to believe otherwise.

62269888
>>62269455
Job market. Who wants to make big purchase when it's so hard find a new job.

62269909
>>62269725
It's not that overpriced $14 for 3(4?) tenders and coleslaw, fries n toast plus drink? That's pretty much any fast food place anymore. The big arch meal was $17>
People are depressed and regular food is expensive as hell too. It used to be buying food at the grocery store was so much cheaper you can't justify it, but now it is only so much cheaper and tastes worse if you suck at cooking, many do.
However the amount of "I'm only eating beans" posting I see makes me think that is also happening.

62269918
>>62269874
Wew lawd to be able to cope this hard, may I have a puff of that?

62270130
>>62269455
because that retarded orange nigger decided to double the debt, rape consumer pockets with his tariffs, then gift them to corpofaggots. Fuck that dumbass, braindead, dipshit, rapist, incompetent, pissant little nigger. Fuck you too cunt.

62270242
>>62269874
fucking doom and gloomers

https://www.tiktok.com/@hog_tok1/video/7308692257761234222

62270350
>>62269455
Because it's time for the crisis

62271678
>>62269614
amerimutts shit themselves and decided to pick the faggot who made everything worse for them instead of voting for the brown bitch kek

62271704
>>62269461
Imagine believing that presidents decide policy in 2026 yikes.

62271707
>>62271678
She still would have attacked Iran. AND trooned out your kids.

62271718
Import millions of tax leeches who drive down wages AND drive up cost of housing, then act surprised when it hurts citizens...

62272054
>>62271678
I live in the most conservative part of a really red state but I've never seen so many "I regret voting for Trump" signs in lawns. Everywhere I look, not one Trump supporter who doesn't regret it. It's crazy because everybody here voted Trump 3 times in a row, but it's obvious that this time, it was a mistake. We call him "Trump the Mistake" in my house.

I was at a bar in town on Saturday night. It's the kind of place where working class guys, truckers, and farmers go to unwind after an honest day's work, a real salt-of-the-Earth type of place. We were watching a local high school football game on the TV and one of the players happened to be named Harris, which prompted people in the bar to start talking about Kamala Harris. My dad (a coal-miner) says "y'know, I've voted red since the Reagan administration but I really should have given her a chance. I'm a fool for being so racist and sexist that I couldn't vote for a Brown woman, she was clearly the best choice." Then the local gun store owner, who is also the head of the local KKK chapter, said “I can’t believe I’m saying this, but we need to impeach Trump and Vance and replace them with Kamala.” I look around and my face is stunned. All of these hardcore Trump supporters are shaking their heads in agreement. I heard a couple “yes, sir” and “damn straight”. Everyone started nodding and agreeing, and we were talking about how she's not a billionaire so she understands the plight of the middle class. We all call her Middle Class Kamala around these parts.

She was the best candidate for the job, and the only reason she lost is because we were too racist and sexist to realize it. She was basically President for the last 4 years and our economy was booming. We call her Queen Kamala and praise her Kamalaconomy. Anyways just thought I'd throw in my two cents on the matter

62272889
>>62269526
It’s shocking (not really) how this is always brushed under the rug. We just get told “Covid caused inflation” but never the monetary expansion root of it. What exactly were they trying to cover up with that $40 trillion?

62272900
>>62272054
Lmao is this the new copy pasta?

62272903
>>62272054
good pasta, cooked al dente

62272976
>>62269874
The internet IS the real world now. We're past the point where you can ignore it. It is tied so deeply with every aspect of our society that it couldnt function without it.

62273065
>>62269614
Kamala Harris
Donald Trump
Jill Stein
Chase Oliver
Cornel West
Claudia De la Cruz
Randall Terry
Peter Sonski

God, americans are so dumb. Open your goddamn eyes sheep.

62273072
>>62269461
So much this fellow Kamala xister

62273084
>>62269455
why dont they just lie about consumer sentiment? They already fabricate every other stat
>>62269461
The economy was shit under Biden, and its shit under Trump. Votes dont do shit.

item.title: FIRE is mental illness — 259 replies — 16.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
FIRE is mental illness

Open thread • Replies: 259 • Updated: 2026-05-25 09:08 UTC

OP

No woman, no kids, $25k YEARLY spend.
You're not supposed to check out of society in your 30s and spend the rest of your life relaxing. What is wrong with modern men?

Early replies (first 3)

62259938
>>62259935
Keep slaving away goyman

62259945
>>62259935
Nice bait thread faggot

62259968
>>62259935
You can't really afford to marry on FIRE because one divorce ruins a decade+ of saving and then you have to work again. If a woman finds out you're FIRE she will absolutely divorce rape you too since the wealth disparity between you and her is so great she'll walk away with a ludicrous amount of money.

Huge downside of FIRE, you can never marry.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62265888
>>62259935
You know you can easily have biological children without marriage right?

Just fucking cum in some young woman and keep doing it until ahe misses a period. If that makes you fags squeamish just pay some surrogate to carry your child while you enjoy escorts or casual dating and sex.

It really isn't that hard, and after the surrogate pushes your child out it'll be the last time you need to hear a woman yell at you ever again lol.

62265896
>>62264378
>This is not the person I would mock, he actually did fucking make it. I have no idea how you get 12 years in work make near 2 million and also a fully paid house jesus christ wow.
the stemfag market was goated 12 years ago, now you have to be the throte goat to find anything

62265903
>>62265888
My gf keeps eating the cum before it reaches her pussy though.

62265928
>>62265903
Wrong hole.
Blowjobs are nice but shoot for a simultaneous orgasm. By far the best sensation the human body can experience and it makes women more loyal to you (if they can feel such a thing).

62268369
>>62263653
>>62264007
Eh, it's still hot though. You get bored after 10 years of fucking 1 woman.

62268422
>>62259968
>FIRE
>implying it's the end
There's no reason you can't still invest. In fact, you should considering how many unexpected expenses can pop up unless you're filthy rich once you're 'done' with FIRE.

62268475
>>62259968
>Huge downside of FIRE, you can never marry.
>never marry
>downside

62268693
>>62259935
It's kind of odd to me that people with such a net worth only ever consider retiring to do nothing.
A more natural progression seems to be managing your own wealth. When your net worth is so high you can only grow it by 5-10% per year, improving your portfolio's performance by a few % (not only from growth but also downside protection/risk management) has a huge impact.
Many probably buy into the "nothing beats buying and holding the index forever" narrative, and many don't want to bother dealing with finance things, but at some wealth:salary ratio it has to become unreasonable to avoid it.

62268709
>>62265928
the majority of porn/internet addicted retards don't even know this is possible. You have to be in tune together for this to happen and that's not possible if you're of a low vibration

62269694
>>62268709
It's pretty easy if you let her use a wand or clit sucking vibrator to give herself orgasms. The only tricky part is YOU cumming during one of her 5 or 8 orgasms.

62269751
>>62259935
>You're not supposed to check out of society in your 30s and spend the rest of your life relaxing
Why not? Are you a woman? It's usually only parasites annoyed by this.

62269890
>incel bait (((mysteriously))) ruins another anti waging thread
this reddit poster is obviously some low test gooner soiboi. In reality 1.9m in an index fund can get you around 150k a year which is more than enough for a non-incel life especially with a paid off house.

62269901
>>62259935
I'm 37 and once I hit a 2m net worth in the next couple of years I'm going to "retire" and go back into academia. It doesn't pay very well but at least the work is enjoyable and I get to spend time with other scientists instead of these bullshit artists in industry.

62269979
>>62259935
No women? The entire point of FIRE is to fuck wives while the husband works...did you even read the manifesto?

62270348
>>62259935
I'm 40 with only ~$400k net worth. No kids/wife/never been on a date. $25k annual spend.
Just get me a rope...

62270366
How dare you??
You are just a damn child.

62270374
>>62270348
I had $400k net worth when I was 40. I'm 44 now with $1.7 million net worth. A lot can change in a short amount of time.

62270394
>>62270374
>A lot can change in a short amount of time.
Bitcoin's growth is slower now and that's my only hope.
My salary is currently $100k and I don't see potential for any significant raises in my future.
At least I think I'll have the balls to exit the system at $1M and maybe find a part time job stocking shelves to keep me busy.

62270406
>>62270348
You're not missing anything. Society is fucked. I didn't start dating until I was ~33. One woman that I dated when I was 34 ruined me forever. Not financially - just mentally.

I'm worse off financially than you because of student loans but in a few more years, I'll be in better shape albeit still with like half of your net worth when I hit your age. You're in okay shape. You're doing much better than most of your peers. You can get the validation from a woman if you push for it but it won't help. I got validation from other women after the crazy one and I didn't feel a thing. Hopefully you have nieces and nephews for whom you can leave something behind. That's my plan even though I'll probably never even meet my nieces or nephews. So it goes.

62270414
>>62270406
Thanks, bro. I've got my music, I guess.
>nieces and nephews
Nah. I'm a single child raised by a single mother. Once she's gone, I'm all alone.

62270422
>>62270414
Sorry man. My nieces and nephews are the children of my cousins, not any siblings, and I don't have a relationship with my cousins. For all intents and purposes, I'll be pretty alone when my parents are gone and they're both up there in age.

Music is my thing too. I don't enjoy it as much anymore though. Nothing hits the same after that woman. You still have a lot of time to find someone especially since you have your shit together financially but just be careful. I don't want anyone to ever have to feel the way I do on account of a woman.

62270444
>>62270422
I hope things will work out for us in the end.
I'd definitely rather be in a band (something like Jaga Jazzist) than a relationship :) We'll see were things go.

62270945
>>62262891

OnlyFans is always hiring: no experience necessary, work from home at your own hours, no dress code required

62271089
>>62259968
so dont marry. i literally do not understand why you would if you have money and a house?
horny? hire a prostitute.
lonely? get friends, or see above.
existential crisis? realize its fake and gay, we all go into the ground eventually. dead and rotting in the ground.

62271100
>>62270366
i thought the chinese have a huge surplus of men
my mom got pregnant when she was 40 and had me. i only have a touch of 'tism

62271108
>>62268693
All the FIREs are also all bogles. They do not have the brains or cojones to try to get a +3%/year outperformance on the SPY (which I get easily in one of my bad but green months). They do not trust themselves that grand feat.

62271141
>>62263178
Does your "t" button not work?

62271142
>>62261104

62271171
>>62269901

62271254

item.title: I won about 8 LTC from a crypto casino — 18 replies — 14.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
I won about 8 LTC from a crypto casino

Open thread • Replies: 18 • Updated: 2026-05-26 00:07 UTC

OP

I have 15 in total now.. I am gonna hodl them.

Early replies (first 3)

62270209
>>62270028
Swap for XMR and hodl

62270220
>>62270209
Thats my plan but Monero's price is just too high right now. So, I will just wait and see for now. LTC is near its this years low, so it makes sense holding it or even buying more of it.

62271797
aim for 50 at least, this coin is going to power the decentralized AI agent economy

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62271814
sooooo who is going to tell him?

62272140
>>62270028
I have 800

62272349
no one mentions MWEB which is one solid reason to be bullish on LTC

62272353
>>62272349
qrd?
Also what is the make it and sui stack for this?

62272539
>>62272353
It's a lightweight privacy extension, essentially. It hides the amount and receiver. It doesn't hide the sender though (though there are some tools like Hector Chu's coinjoin that would essentially do this if people used it.) Make it stack on LTC has always been 84. Same as having 21 BTC since it has a maximum supply of 84 million.

As for SUI, be very careful of VC coins people shill. Lots of insiders staking the premine waiting to dump it. I'm not saying don't buy it, just be aware of that. There will come a day when people that got it for free (including people marketing it, which is probably how you heard about it someone marketing it getting paid in the coin and not disclosing they got a shitload of them for free). Not saying they can't pump, but they can dump very hard and never recover. Fun to swing trade though.

62272587
>>62272539
How many LTC or XMR do you have sensei?

62272618
>>62272587
I stopped buying XMR when it went to $300. It could potentially be the most valuable coin, but it has so many liquidity channels cut off I'm happy with where I'm at. You should probably treat the make it stack as 21.

Both the qubic event in XMR and inflation bug in LTC (even though it was caught and didn't do permanent damage) have made me weary on privacy coins. I think you should have them, but BTC should be your core stack if you're new. If I were just entering the market, I'd probably pick up an LTC stack first since it's so cheap right now, then an XMR stack. Not necessarily a make it stack, but just something so you don't FOMO if they pump. Since BTC.d is so high, it'd pick up the alts for maybe a year (just so you don't fomo them later) and then never buy them again and focus on accumulating BTC at any price. I might be wrong and BTC just accelerates higher, but I don't think it'll go so high so fast it'll make much of a difference if you pick up the alts first. 1st generation PoW is pretty ignored right now except BTC, so they are probably safe bets. Of course I'll keep my on stack amounts a secret. Nothing good can come from telling anyone.

62272639
>>62272618
Thanks, I appreciate the advice :)

62272654
>>62272618
do you think xmr could realistically do a huge X from where it is?

62272668
>>62272654
Yeah. BTC ran to 20k in 2017 on retail fomo essentially. There has been a huge increase in the global money supply since then. Honestly, BTC could go to like 700k and it wouldn't surprise me, XMR could go to 4k and it wouldn't surprise me and LTC could go to $800 and it wouldn't surprise me. All this shit is undervalued. Another thing is fiat always dies. All fiat currencies have died historically and the governments of the world are doing the same thing that has killed all fiat currencies historically. It's just a matter of when. In the mean time though, we still have to live in a world where we settle in fiat, so don't invest more than you can afford to lose. Just treat these things as extra savings accounts.

62272838
>>62271797
BULLSHIT

62272845
>>62272539
No one said a fucking thing about sui retarded nigger he asked what a suicide stack was.

62272863
>>62271814
Who cares? Swap vs keep. It’s still a lose-lose bet in all altcoins atp, even Ai agrees lmao

62272971
>>62272845
It specifically says sui you dumb nigger. Not that guys fault you guys speak in nigger babble slang rather than typing it out.

62273235
>>62272863
Yeah Bitcoin is the only way to not loose money, why even have a bank account just keep all your life savings in bitcoin, since it's so stable and basically a guaranteed profite. You go first anon.

item.title: Just finished my first year of law school and I did really well, so I'm basically a lawyer now. — 18 replies — 14.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
Just finished my first year of law school and I did really well, so I'm basically a lawyer now.

Open thread • Replies: 18 • Updated: 2026-05-26 00:06 UTC

OP

Just finished my first year of law school and I did really well, so I'm basically a lawyer now. AMA law related /biz/ questions you may have.

Early replies (first 3)

62272819
>>62272812
Can i peepee in the poopoo or will i go to jail?

62272833
>>62272819
You may

62272835
>>62272812
Is it legal for a president to collab with a news outlet to pump & dump the stock market while insiders make bank on it?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62272848
>>62272812
Why are you brown?

62272849
>>62272835
No but very tough to prove or prosecute

62272858
>>62272835
You don't get to complain about this after Biden. Sorry. Thats just how it works.

62272965
>>62272848
I'm not brown, I'm Irish-American

62273031
>>62272812
gg Anon

62273035
>>62272848
why do white niggers assume everyone who went to college or grad school is brown or a chink? should have paid attention in class, dumbfuck.

62273041
>>62272965
the irish people have done nothing for humanity, and neither will you. You are niggers compared to Russians. Afrikkkan niggers have accomplished more.

62273047
do they teach all law students how to act in court, even if you don’t become the kind of lawyer who has to go to court?

62273090
>>62273047
First year was very basic, we had one oral argument in our legal writing class and that was it. Other than that all classes were just like normal undergrad classes, just law related. There is optional stuff like a Mock Trial tournament every year but they don't make people do that.

62273152
>>62272812
>AMA law related
How much upwards mobility do you think a law degree gives someone? I heard that the rich kids are setup by their parents with the chancery and all while the poor kids struggle to make mich out of their degree.

62273154
>>62272812
what will happen with chainlink on 6/6/26?

62273163
>>62273152
Well I haven't graduated yet, but employment data for my school is really good. It all depends on where you go and how well you do it seems.
>>62273154
It will skyrocket from $9.44 to $9.45

62273171
does it seem like most of your classmates are smart? I was at a high school reunion recently and spoke to a guy who loafed around doing menial work for like 15 years after college. then he took the lsat and got into Northwestern. he has a big time job now defending people in trouble with the SEC.

62273174
>>62273171
Yeah, how many law students are just dumbfucks?

62273234
Junior lawyer is literally technician in legal system. Very far from management and get treated like disposable human trash
